features.title: Bootstrap minimal AI agent harness template
features.description: Create the harness state files, schema, prompts, validation scripts, and tiny example needed to prove the template works end to end.
acceptance: ./init.sh validates required files and feature_list.json
scripts/validate-feature.sh F001 validates the selected feature
scripts/summarize-progress.sh prints feature totals and next work
examples/tiny-cli test coverage passes without third-party dependencies

features.title: Add vendor-neutral lightweight orchestrator
features.description: Add a generic orchestrator that follows the startup protocol, selects one unfinished feature, previews or runs Coding Agent and Evaluator Agent prompts, supports eval-only mode, and keeps vendor-specific agent execution configurable.
acceptance: orchestrator.py exists at the template root
python3 orchestrator.py --dry-run runs the startup protocol and exits successfully when there is no unfinished feature
python3 orchestrator.py --eval-only F001 --dry-run previews the evaluator prompt
./init.sh validates orchestrator.py syntax
README.md and SPEC.md document the orchestrator boundary

features.title: Adopt practiced AGENTS guardrails
features.description: Refine the template AGENTS.md using proven guardrails from the production agent workflow, including initializer responsibilities, state safety rules, external behavior verification, external tool schema rules, work rules, anti-patterns, and recoverability goals.
acceptance: AGENTS.md includes an Initializer role for bootstrapping repository state
AGENTS.md includes state safety rules for preserving feature_list.json fields and ordering
AGENTS.md includes external behavior verification rules for CLI, API, runtime, and structured tool output assumptions
AGENTS.md includes anti-patterns that prevent premature completion and chat-history reliance
./init.sh passes after the guardrail update

features.title: Add testing layers to the harness template
features.description: Add an explicit test plan and dependency-free unit, contract, and smoke test layers to the template, wire them into ./init.sh, and document how downstream projects should extend the layers.
acceptance: test_plan.md documents the verification entry point and unit, contract, harness, and smoke layers
test/unit contains tests for validation and progress helper behavior
test/contract contains tests that lock AGENTS, feature schema, prompts, and orchestrator contracts
test/smoke contains an end-to-end smoke test for the template commands
./init.sh runs unit, contract, smoke, and optional harness tests

features.title: Strengthen contract tests for AI harness obligations
features.description: Refine contract tests so they verify AI agent obligations and harness boundaries, including external behavior verification, feature state preservation, prompt restrictions, evaluator anti-premature-completion rules, and orchestrator state-transition contracts.
acceptance: Contract tests verify AGENTS external behavior verification requirements beyond section presence
Contract tests verify feature state fields, uniqueness, done/pass consistency, and unknown-field preservation requirements
Contract tests verify work, continue, and evaluate prompts constrain AI behavior and state mutation
Contract tests verify orchestrator state-transition and eval-only boundaries by static inspection
./init.sh passes after strengthening contract coverage

features.title: Replace generic agent command with role adapters
features.description: Remove the generic HARNESS_AGENT_COMMAND orchestration hook and make orchestrator.py dispatch explicit Coding Agent and Evaluator Agent role adapters instead.
acceptance: orchestrator.py no longer references HARNESS_AGENT_COMMAND
orchestrator.py dispatches scripts/run-coding-agent.sh for Coding Agent prompts
orchestrator.py dispatches scripts/run-evaluator-agent.sh for Evaluator Agent prompts
README.md, SPEC.md, and AGENTS.md document role adapters instead of a generic agent command
./init.sh passes after the adapter boundary change

features.title: Add repository knowledge map, quality rubric, and run artifacts
features.description: Add a docs knowledge map, evaluator quality rubric, and run artifact mechanism so the harness captures repository-local knowledge, evaluator criteria, and per-run evidence without bloating AGENTS.md or progress.md.
acceptance: docs/README.md indexes architecture, testing, external behavior, workflow, and decision-log documents
QUALITY.md defines evaluator rubric criteria and pass/fail guidance
runs/ contains a run log template and scripts/summarize-runs.sh summarizes recorded runs
AGENTS.md points agents to docs/ and QUALITY.md instead of becoming the only knowledge source
Evaluator prompt requires applying QUALITY.md and recording run evidence when appropriate
./init.sh validates the new knowledge, quality, and run artifact files

features.title: Add failure-domain to harness-improvement loop
features.description: Add a mechanism that classifies failures by domain and forces failed or blocked run records to assess whether the harness itself needs improvement through docs, prompts, scripts, schemas, tests, or follow-up features.
acceptance: docs/failure-domains.md defines failure domains and the improvement loop
runs/RUN_TEMPLATE.md includes Failure Analysis fields for failure domain, summary, harness improvement, and follow-up feature
scripts/check-failure-domains.sh fails when failed run records omit domain or harness improvement assessment
orchestrator.py writes a failed run record when unattended coding or evaluation fails
AGENTS.md and prompts require agents to classify failures and turn harness weaknesses into durable improvements
./init.sh runs the failure-domain check
Contract, unit, and smoke tests cover the failure-domain mechanism

features.title: Add Go server example
features.description: Add a dependency-free Go HTTP server example that demonstrates how the harness can validate service-style projects with standard library tests.
acceptance: examples/go-server contains a Go module with a small HTTP server
The server exposes GET /healthz and GET /greet?name=Codex endpoints
The Go server example has tests covering health, greeting, blank-name defaulting, and method rejection
./init.sh verifies the Go server example with go test ./... when Go is installed
README.md documents how to test and run the Go server example

features.title: Add Makefile and GitHub Actions CI
features.description: Add a Makefile that standardizes local harness commands and a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the same CI verification path on push and pull request.
acceptance: Makefile defines init, test, validate, unit, contract, smoke, go-example, dry-run, summarize, and ci targets
make ci runs ./init.sh, feature validation, and orchestrator dry-run
.github/workflows/ci.yml runs make ci on push, pull_request, and workflow_dispatch
README.md documents Makefile usage
./init.sh validates the Makefile and GitHub Actions workflow are present

features.title: Explore concurrent agent execution
features.description: If future workflows need higher throughput, explore a bounded concurrent-agent mode that can run independent Coding Agent or Evaluator Agent work without violating feature state safety, run evidence, or evaluator gating.
acceptance: Concurrency design defines which feature states may run in parallel and which must remain serialized
Orchestrator concurrency preserves one-writer semantics for feature_list.json and progress.md
Run records make concurrent work attributable to a feature, agent role, and attempt
Evaluator gating remains per feature and cannot be bypassed by concurrent execution
Tests cover race-prone state transitions before concurrent mode is enabled

features.title: Improve README positioning and onboarding
features.description: Rewrite the README first screen and quick start so new users understand the project as a repository-level harness for resumable AI coding rather than a prompt collection.
acceptance: README opens with Make AI coding projects resumable
README explains the concrete failure modes: interrupted sessions, long context, quota exhaustion, forgotten decisions, unrelated edits, and premature completion
README states that the project is not a prompt collection
README separates Use This Template from Verify This Repository
README explains that the orchestrator is intentionally boring and only enforces workflow boundaries
Contract tests lock the README positioning and onboarding language

features.title: Add OSS readiness files and repository metadata
features.description: Add public OSS readiness files and repository metadata so the project is easier to discover, evaluate, contribute to, and maintain.
acceptance: LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, and CHANGELOG.md are present
GitHub issue templates exist for bug reports and feature requests
./init.sh validates the OSS readiness files are present
Contract tests verify OSS readiness files and issue templates
GitHub repository description and topics are set for discoverability

features.title: Add clean-state reset command
features.description: Add a make clean workflow that resets project-specific harness state after copying the template into a new project while preserving the harness files, docs, scripts, tests, examples, and run template.
acceptance: Makefile defines a clean target that calls scripts/clean-state.py
scripts/clean-state.py resets feature_list.json to an empty features array
scripts/clean-state.py resets progress.md to a fresh-project state
scripts/clean-state.py removes recorded run artifacts while preserving runs/RUN_TEMPLATE.md and runs/.gitkeep
README.md documents running make clean after copying the template
./init.sh validates scripts/clean-state.py is present
Unit and contract tests cover clean-state reset behavior

features.title: Document real-world usage references
features.description: Document the real projects that informed the harness design and link them from README and the repository knowledge map without copying full project code into examples.
acceptance: docs/real-world-usage.md links home-guard-tg and agent-remote-tg
docs/real-world-usage.md explains the harness lessons extracted from those projects
README.md links docs/real-world-usage.md and both real project repositories
docs/README.md indexes real-world-usage.md
./init.sh validates docs/real-world-usage.md is present
Contract tests cover real-world usage references

features.title: Link announcement article from README
features.description: Link the public announcement article from README so visitors can understand the story and motivation behind the harness.
acceptance: README.md includes an Announcement section near the top
README.md links the public announcement article
Contract tests verify the announcement link remains present

features.title: Add AI Agent Harness skill
features.description: Add a distributable skill that helps users initialize or repair the harness in a project, follow planning, one-feature implementation, evaluation, and explicit finalize-and-commit workflows while preserving repository files as the durable source of state.
acceptance: skills/ai-agent-harness/SKILL.md defines the skill trigger and keeps repository state as the durable source of truth
The skill includes a deterministic initializer that supports new, adopt, repair, and check modes without overwriting conflicting project files by default
The skill documents planning, one-feature work, evaluator verification, and explicit finalize-and-commit workflows
The finalize-and-commit workflow requires explicit user satisfaction before staging or committing
README.md and SPEC.md document the skill as a convenience layer that preserves the vendor-neutral harness protocol
./init.sh validates the skill files and tests cover the skill contract

features.title: Strengthen harness skill initializer tests and manifest semantics
features.description: Strengthen the AI Agent Harness skill initializer so new, adopt, repair, and check modes are tested against realistic project boundaries, check output distinguishes normal project drift from conflicts, and installed projects record manifest metadata for future version drift handling.
acceptance: Tests cover new, adopt, repair, and check modes for the skill initializer
Tests verify existing project files are not overwritten by default and force overwrite remains explicit
Tests verify repair mode restores missing harness-owned files while preserving project-owned state
Check mode reports missing files, conflicts, drift, installed version, template version, state validity, and next action guidance
The initializer records template and installation manifests that classify files as harness-owned static, project-owned state, merge-sensitive, or optional integration
A newly initialized project is verified as a runnable harness by semantic validation and the harness verification entry point, not only by file existence
README.md and SPEC.md document manifest-based drift handling and the stronger installed-harness definition
./init.sh passes with the expanded initializer tests

features.title: Clarify installed skill usage in README
features.description: Clarify the README and skill documentation so users understand the difference between installing and invoking the AI Agent Harness skill, using the repository checkout's Python helper manually, and using the workflow pack from other agent tools.
acceptance: README.md has a skill installation section that explains installing `skills/ai-agent-harness/` into a skill directory and restarting the agent surface when applicable
README.md shows an installed-skill invocation such as `Use $ai-agent-harness to initialize this project`
README.md separates installed skill usage from manual `python3 skills/ai-agent-harness/scripts/init_harness.py` fallback usage
README.md explains that manual Python commands are for repository checkout or vendor-neutral fallback usage, not the primary installed-skill experience
SKILL.md and workflow references remain consistent with the installed-skill and manual-script distinction
Contract tests verify the clarified README and skill documentation
./init.sh passes after the documentation update

features.title: Document portable cross-agent skill installation
features.description: Update skill installation documentation to remove machine-specific absolute paths and describe portable Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and manual fallback entry points.
acceptance: README.md uses `~` or project-relative paths instead of machine-specific absolute paths for skill installation examples
README.md documents Codex installation with `~/.codex/skills` and the Codex skill installer helper
README.md documents Claude Code installation using personal `~/.claude/skills/ai-agent-harness/` and project `.claude/skills/ai-agent-harness/` skill locations
README.md documents Cursor usage through project rules under `.cursor/rules` and notes the manual initializer script remains the portable fallback
SKILL.md and workflow references avoid machine-specific absolute paths and remain consistent with the cross-agent entry points
Contract tests verify the portable paths and cross-agent documentation
./init.sh passes after the documentation update

features.title: Add capability gap governance
features.description: Strengthen the harness and distributable skill so agents must turn missing tools, permissions, generators, dependencies, services, credentials, runtime settings, CI resources, and verification fixtures into durable project capabilities or tracked blocked/follow-up work instead of bypassing them with local-only workarounds.
acceptance: AGENTS.md defines capability gap handling and forbids bypassing required missing capabilities with hand-written generated artifacts, weakened scope, skipped verification, or local-only environment changes
docs/capability-gaps.md documents the required response, workaround rules, examples, and evaluator expectations for missing capabilities
Planning, coding, continuation, and evaluation prompts require agents to identify, record, and resolve capability gaps durably or track them as blocked/follow-up work
QUALITY.md and docs/failure-domains.md include capability-gap evaluation and failure classification
The skill initializer and template manifest include the capability-gap documentation so newly initialized projects receive the rule
Contract tests verify the capability-gap governance remains present
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Add default example boundaries
features.description: Strengthen the harness and distributable skill so agents treat default examples as demonstration fixtures instead of implementing project-level requirements inside examples/tiny-cli, examples/go-server, or other example paths.
acceptance: AGENTS.md defines example boundaries and forbids satisfying project-level requirements by repurposing default examples unless the selected feature explicitly targets examples
docs/example-boundaries.md documents that examples are harness demonstrations, gives project-owned path guidance, and includes protobuf/gRPC-style project work as a non-example implementation case
Planning, coding, continuation, and evaluation prompts require agents to identify project-owned implementation paths and reject project work implemented only in examples
QUALITY.md and docs/failure-domains.md include example-boundary evaluation and failure classification
README.md clarifies that default examples may be removed or replaced during setup but should not become the product implementation surface
The skill initializer and bundled template include the example-boundary documentation so newly initialized projects receive the rule
Contract tests verify the example-boundary governance remains present
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Add hidden harness installation layout
features.description: Add installation layout profiles so user projects can keep root-level business code visible while storing harness state, prompts, docs, scripts, tests, runs, schemas, and examples under .agent-harness by default, with visible layout retained for template maintenance.
acceptance: The skill initializer accepts `--layout hidden` and `--layout visible`, defaults new installs to hidden, and records the selected layout in `.agent-harness/manifest.json`
Hidden layout creates thin root `AGENTS.md` and `init.sh` entry points while storing the harness body under `.agent-harness/`
Hidden layout `./init.sh`, check, repair, and quick semantic validation succeed without root-level `feature_list.json`, `progress.md`, `prompts/`, `runs/`, `scripts/`, or harness tests
Visible layout remains available for harness development and preserves the existing root-level template shape
README.md, SPEC.md, SKILL.md, and workflow references document layout profiles and the hidden default
Contract and harness tests verify hidden layout, visible layout, manifest layout metadata, and version drift diagnostics
The bundled skill template is synchronized with the layout-profile implementation
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Add feature decomposition governance
features.description: Strengthen Planning Agent, Evaluator Agent, and skill guidance so broad user requirements are decomposed into independently verifiable feature entries instead of being collapsed into one over-bundled feature.
acceptance: AGENTS.md requires Planning Agents to decompose broad requirements and forbids collapsing unrelated or independently verifiable work into one feature
docs/feature-decomposition.md defines split triggers, allowed merges, required planning output, and evaluator expectations
Planning and evaluation prompts require use of docs/feature-decomposition.md and reject generic all-requirements or over-bundled features
QUALITY.md and docs/failure-domains.md include feature-decomposition evaluation and failure classification
README.md, SPEC.md, docs/agent-workflow.md, SKILL.md, and workflow references document the decomposition rule
The skill initializer and bundled template include the feature-decomposition documentation so newly initialized projects receive the rule
Contract tests verify the feature-decomposition governance remains present
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Add feature-linked commit message rules
features.description: Require approved feature commits to include their feature ID in a predictable commit subject format so git history can be correlated with feature_list.json for analysis and harness improvement.
acceptance: docs/commit-messages.md defines the feature commit subject format, batch feature handling, non-feature commit handling, and finalize checklist
AGENTS.md and the skill finalize-and-commit workflow require commit subjects to include approved feature IDs
README.md, SPEC.md, QUALITY.md, and docs/agent-workflow.md document feature-linked commit traceability
The skill initializer and bundled template include docs/commit-messages.md so newly initialized projects receive the rule
Contract tests verify commit-message governance remains present
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Preserve executable script modes during installation
features.description: Fix the skill initializer so hidden and visible harness installs make required shell entrypoints executable even when the installed skill template lost executable bits during download or extraction.
acceptance: The initializer defines required executable harness paths and chmods them after copying or generating files
Hidden layout installs produce executable root `init.sh`, `.agent-harness/init.sh`, and `.agent-harness/scripts/*.sh` entrypoints
Semantic validation checks all required shell entrypoints for executable bits instead of only a subset
Harness tests simulate a template source with non-executable shell scripts and verify the installed harness is runnable
The bundled skill template is synchronized with the executable-bit fix
Contract tests verify the initializer keeps executable-bit handling logic
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Separate harness verification from project recovery init
features.description: Strengthen the harness initialization flow so installed projects distinguish the internal harness health check from the root project recovery entry point, and require root init.sh to become a real dependency install, service startup, and smoke-test contract once a minspec is accepted.
acceptance: AGENTS.md and docs define `.agent-harness/scripts/init.sh` as harness verification and root `./init.sh` as the project recovery entry point
Initialization guidance states that before a minspec exists, root `./init.sh` may only verify the harness and must not claim project business behavior is runnable

Planning guidance requires the first post-minspec project feature to create or adapt a runnable skeleton and update root `./init.sh` to install dependencies, start required services, and run a real smoke test
Project recovery init requirements include clear logs, idempotency, no manual steps, no assumption that services are already running, and non-zero exit on failure
README.md, SPEC.md, SKILL.md, workflow references, and bundled template files stay consistent for hidden and visible layouts
Contract and harness tests verify the project recovery init governance remains present
./init.sh passes after the update

features.title: Add minspec-to-SPEC normalization governance
features.description: Require Planning Agent work to normalize new requirements into explicit goal, included scope, excluded scope, core flows, constraints, ambiguity handling, required capabilities, implementation paths, and verification surfaces before appending feature entries.
acceptance: AGENTS.md requires Planning Agents to normalize requirements before feature decomposition

docs/spec-normalization.md defines required SPEC fields: goal, included scope, excluded scope, core flows, constraints, ambiguities or assumptions, required capabilities, implementation paths, and verification surface
prompts/plan.md rejects vague requirements instead of turning them directly into feature entries
docs/agent-workflow.md, QUALITY.md, docs/failure-domains.md, SKILL.md, and workflow references document planner and evaluator expectations for spec normalization
The bundled skill template includes spec-normalization governance so newly initialized projects receive the rule
Contract tests verify the spec-normalization requirements for AGENTS.md, docs, prompts, quality, failure domains, skill docs, and bundled template files
./init.sh passes after the governance update

features.title: Require evaluator evidence before completion
features.description: Add a durable guardrail that fails verification when features at or after the evaluator-evidence baseline are marked done without a run record containing the matching evaluator pass line.
acceptance: AGENTS.md and docs define evaluator evidence as required before a feature may be marked done
A verification script checks done features at or after the enforcement baseline for matching `EVAL_PASS: Fxxx` run evidence
./init.sh runs the evaluator-evidence check
Unit or contract tests prove the evaluator-evidence check fails for missing pass evidence and passes when evidence exists
Planning, coding, evaluation, progress, README, SKILL.md, workflow references, and bundled template files document the guardrail
The bundled skill template includes the evaluator-evidence guardrail so newly initialized projects receive it
./init.sh and scripts/validate-feature.sh F029 pass after evaluator evidence is recorded

features.title: Make orchestrator the default work entrypoint
features.description: Update the harness so one-feature implementation and evaluation normally begin through the orchestrator, with manual Coding Agent work documented only as an explicit fallback when adapters are unavailable or the user requests interactive/manual work.
acceptance: AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/agent-workflow.md, prompts, SKILL.md, workflow references, and bundled template files describe the orchestrator as the default work entrypoint for one-feature implementation and evaluation
A documented convenience command or target runs one orchestrator round for the next unfinished feature
Manual Coding Agent work is documented as an explicit fallback and must not bypass evaluator gating, evaluator evidence, attempts, failure records, or final ./init.sh verification
Adapter-unavailable flows fail closed with clear guidance instead of silently marking feature state by hand
Contract tests verify the orchestrator-first default entrypoint and manual-fallback boundaries
./init.sh and scripts/validate-feature.sh F030 pass after evaluator evidence is recorded

features.title: Make orchestrator-first agent provider configurable
features.description: Add an explicit provider configuration path for orchestrator role adapters so downstream projects can choose Codex, Claude Code, Cursor Agent, or a custom provider without unsafe implicit guessing.
acceptance: The harness defines a durable provider configuration contract for the Coding Agent and Evaluator Agent adapters
Provider execution is explicit and fails closed when no provider is configured, the configured command is missing, or multiple provider candidates are present without a selection
Codex, Claude Code, Cursor Agent, and custom-provider setup guidance is documented without assuming unverified CLI flags or output schemas
External provider command behavior trusted by the adapters is verified through real help output, official documentation, captured logs, or real-shaped fixtures before being used
Unit or contract tests cover configured provider dispatch, unconfigured provider failure, missing-command failure, and ambiguous-provider handling
README.md, SPEC.md, docs/capability-gaps.md, docs/external-behavior.md, SKILL.md, workflow references, and bundled template files stay consistent
./init.sh and scripts/validate-feature.sh F031 pass after evaluator evidence is recorded

features.title: Document the new-project skill flow
features.description: Add a concise new-project flow guide with a visual diagram so users can see what the AI Agent Harness skill does, what inputs humans must provide, and how initialization, minspec planning, runnable skeleton, provider setup, orchestrator work, evaluation, init verification, and commit approval fit together.
acceptance: docs/new-project-flow.md explains the end-to-end new project flow from skill invocation through approved commit
The guide includes a Mermaid flow diagram that distinguishes skill actions, human inputs, orchestrator work, evaluator gating, and ./init.sh verification
The guide explicitly lists required human inputs: project/install choice, minspec, clarification answers, provider choice, approval to run real agent work, and commit approval
README.md links to docs/new-project-flow.md near the onboarding path
docs/README.md, SPEC.md, scripts/init.sh, contract tests, and bundled template files stay consistent
./init.sh and scripts/validate-feature.sh F032 pass after evaluator evidence is recorded
